contract.title: Will TikTok be banned in the United States by April 30, 2025?
contract.resolution_criteria:
Resolves YES if, on or before 2025-04-30, TikTok is unavailable in the United States
via the Apple App Store or the Google Play Store, OR a federal directive (PAFACA
enforcement order from the Department of Commerce / Department of Justice) takes
effect requiring a divestiture or shutdown. Resolves NO otherwise.

settlement_entities.name: Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States

conditions.label: TikTok absent in US Apple App Store
conditions.summary: On or before 2025-04-30, TikTok is not available to US users in the Apple App Store (unlisted/blocked in the US storefront).

conditions.label: TikTok absent in US Google Play
conditions.summary: On or before 2025-04-30, TikTok is not available to US users in the Google Play Store (unlisted/blocked in the US storefront).

conditions.label: Federal PAFACA order takes effect
conditions.summary: On or before 2025-04-30, a DOC or DOJ PAFACA enforcement order takes effect requiring TikTok/ByteDance to divest or shut down US operations.

contract.narrative: Broader scrutiny followed CFPB remarks in Sept 2024, but the 2025 entries were unrelated state insurance/disciplinary actions with no bearing on TikTok. With no federal PAFACA enforcement or app‑store removals before the deadline, TikTok was not banned by April 30, 2025, and the contract resolved No.

timeline.one_line_why: Alaska Division of Insurance amended order concerns state insurance matters and officials, not TikTok availability or federal PAFACA actions.

timeline.regulator: Department of Commerce, Community, and Economic Development, Division of Insurance — Division of Insurance
timeline.one_line_why: Alaska insurance bulletin concerns state insurance matters, not TikTok, app stores, or federal PAFACA enforcement, so it doesn't affect resolution.

timeline.one_line_why: Alaska Division of Insurance bulletin concerns state insurance matters unrelated to federal app-store availability or PAFACA enforcement, so it doesn't affect TikTok ban odds.

timeline.title: Prepared Opening Remarks of CFPB Director Rohit Chopra at the Aspen Institute on Abuse and Misuse of Our Personal Data
timeline.one_line_why: Consumer Financial Protection Bureau — Consumer Financial Protection Bureau: tangential to resolution, no directional effect.

open_tickets.summary: Escalating CFIUS activity on ByteDance — listing review needed before EOW

primary_drivers: Amended Stipulated Agreement & Order (2025-02-25) signaling active enforcement
Two Jan 24 Bulletins (B25-04, B25-05) issuing new regulatory guidance
Regulatory board disciplinary archive + CFPB commentary indicating sustained oversight
heat_panel.explainer: Heat (60.25) is driven by fresh enforcement and guidance activity — notably the Feb 25 amended stipulated agreement, two Jan bulletins and disciplinary records — with a small 7‑day dip (-2.94).
